repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: AUTHORITATIVE OVERVIEW — before generating anything, read `CLAUDE.md` (project instructions) and `README.md` (quick start + distribution overview) — the canonical, maintainer-written description of ArcKit. They supersede any inference drawn from file structure alone. Prefer paraphrasing from these two files over reconstructing meaning from code.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: CURRENT SIZE — 68 officially-maintained slash commands (the baseline quoted in marketing). From v4.7.0 an optional EU/FR community overlay adds 18 more, for 86 total when enabled. Always cite 68 as the top-line; the extra 18 are counted inside their own community section. ArcKit also ships 10 autonomous research agents, 4 skills, 5 MCP servers, and 62 document templates. Earlier auto-generated wikis cited 57 commands and 6 agents — those numbers are stale.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: SEVEN DISTRIBUTION FORMATS (not four) — (1) Claude Code plugin `arckit-claude/` (canonical), (2) Gemini CLI extension `extensions/arckit-gemini/`, (3) Codex CLI extension `extensions/arckit-codex/`, (4) OpenCode CLI extension `extensions/arckit-opencode/`, (5) Copilot prompt pack `extensions/arckit-copilot/`, (6) Python CLI `src/arckit_cli/` used to scaffold Codex/OpenCode/Copilot projects, (7) TypeScript `paperclip` plugin (72 tools, committed on branch). Older wiki generations listed only four — that is outdated.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH — `plugins/arckit-claude/commands/` and `plugins/arckit-claude/agents/` are canonical. All other formats are generated by `scripts/converter.py` via an `AGENT_CONFIG` dictionary. The converter rewrites `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths, inlines agent prompts for platforms without Task/subagent architecture, strips Claude-only frontmatter fields (`effort`, `maxTurns`, `disallowedTools`, `initialPrompt`, `paths`, `keep-coding-instructions`), generates Codex `config.toml` and per-agent TOML, and emits Gemini TOML. Never describe extension directories as independent implementations — they are build outputs. Edits belong in `plugins/arckit-claude/` and in the converter.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: ORANGE BOOK RISK MODEL — `/arckit:risk` implements HM Treasury Orange Book (2023) risk management. The Orange Book defines SIX treatment options, not the popular '4Ts' (Tolerate / Treat / Transfer / Terminate). ArcKit's risk command uses the correct six-option model together with the Three Lines of Defence and cascade analysis. An earlier version described '4Ts'; that was a factual error caught by ArcKit's own autoresearch system and corrected. Do not describe the risk command as using '4Ts' or as having four treatment options.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: GREEN BOOK BUSINESS CASE — `/arckit:sobc` generates a Strategic Outline Business Case following HM Treasury Green Book (2026) five-case structure: Strategic, Economic, Commercial, Financial, Management. Use the Green Book's own vocabulary: `BAU` (not 'status quo'), `Do Minimum` (not 'baseline'), `Preferred Way Forward` (not 'recommended option'). Do not rename these terms.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: PLATFORM REQUIREMENTS — the Claude Code plugin requires Claude Code v2.1.112 or later (current floor, bumped in v4.6.7). Plugin and CLI versions track independently: CLI 4.7.1, plugin 4.7.1. `scripts/bump-version.sh` updates 15 version files in one pass (was 13 before Paperclip). Earlier wiki generations cited 11 version files and Claude Code v2.1.71 — both stale.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: WARDLEY MAPPING — the Wardley suite is five commands, not one: `wardley.value-chain`, `wardley`, `wardley.doctrine`, `wardley.climate`, `wardley.gameplay`. The main `wardley` command emits dual output (OWM syntax for create.wardleymaps.ai plus Mermaid `wardley-beta` blocks). Validated against 147 real-world maps from Simon Wardley's public repository with 98% pass rate; 100% of ArcKit-generated syntax is valid (the 3 failing maps are source-data errors in the originals). Do not describe Wardley support as a single command.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: GOVERNMENT CODE DISCOVERY — for UK Government projects, three commands use the `govreposcrape` MCP server over 24,500+ UK public-sector repositories: `/arckit:gov-code-search`, `/arckit:gov-reuse`, `/arckit:gov-landscape`. These feed into the main research command as a fifth build-vs-buy option ('Reuse existing government code'). Distinct from cloud-vendor research commands (`aws-research`, `azure-research`, `gcp-research`) which use AWS Knowledge MCP, Microsoft Learn MCP, and Google Developer Knowledge MCP.

repo_notes.author: ArcKit maintainers
repo_notes.content: WHAT ARCKIT IS NOT — ArcKit is not a diagramming tool (like Structurizr or ArchiMate), a requirements database (like Jama or DOORS), or a wiki. It is a prompt-driven governance automation system that uses AI coding assistants as document-generation engines, guided by 50–200-line prompt files stored as Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Differentiators: template-driven consistency, traceability by construction (every command reads upstream artifacts), real-time MCP-backed research (not training-data lookups), multi-platform distribution from one source, and a self-improving autoresearch system that optimises prompts against real government framework content.
